title: Ways to Describe Emotions - FEAR
description: Comprehensive guide to understanding fear emotions, their triggers, manifestations, and effects

promptingEvents.description: Events that trigger feelings of fear
events: Having your life, your health, or your wellbeing threatened
Being in the same situation (or a similar one) where you have been threatened or gotten hurt in the past, or where painful things have happened
Being in situations where you have seen others threatened or be hurt
Being in a new or unfamiliar situation
Being alone (e.g., walking alone, being home alone, living alone)
Having to perform in front of others

interpretations.description: Interpretations of events that prompt feelings of fear
interpretations: Believing that you might die, or you are going to die
Believing that you might be hurt or harmed
Believing that you might lose something valuable
Believing that someone might reject, criticize, or dislike you
Believing that you will embarrass yourself
Believing that failure is possible; expecting to fail
Believing that you will not get help you want or need
Believing that you might lose help you already have
Believing that you might lose someone important
Believing that you might lose something you want
Believing that you are helpless or are losing a sense of control
Believing that you are incompetent or are losing mastery

biologicalChanges.description: Biological changes and experiences of fear
changes: Urge to scream or call out
Feeling your hairs standing on end
Wanting to run away or avoid things

behaviors: Hiding from or avoiding what you fear
Darting eyes or quickly looking around
Talking yourself out of doing what you fear
Freezing, or trying not to move

effects: Losing your ability to focus or becoming disoriented or dazed
Imagining the possibility of more loss or failure
